---
title: 'How to write a page in Markdown'
---

Standard GitHub Pages can transform simple Markdown files (plain text files with a `.md` extension) into HTML webpages. For this wiki, our setup goes a step further, allowing Markdown to seamlessly handle complex equations, cross-referencing, and bibliography management.

Markdown syntax is highly intuitive. Below are examples of the formatting you will use most often, showing the literal syntax you need to use followed by the final rendered version.

### Headers

Section names are created by adding one or more `#` symbols followed by a space. Use `##` or `###` for subsections and sub-subsections, respectively.

```markdown
# This is a section
## This is a subsection
### This is a sub-subsection
```

# This is a section
## This is a subsection
### This is a sub-subsection

### Mathematical Equations

You can write inline equations by enclosing the formula in single `$` symbols. To write a standalone (unnumbered) equation on its own line, enclose it in double `$$` symbols. 

If you want to create a labeled equation that you can reference later, append `{#eq-yourlabel}` to the closing `$$`. You can then reference it seamlessly using `@eq-yourlabel`.

```markdown
The area of a circle is $A = \pi r^2$.

Here is the quadratic formula:
$$
x=\frac{-b\pm \sqrt{b^2-4ac}}{2a} \,
$$ {#eq-quadratic}

As seen in @eq-quadratic, there are two solutions.
```

The area of a circle is $A = \pi r^2$.

Here is the quadratic formula:
$$
x=\frac{-b\pm \sqrt{b^2-4ac}}{2a} \,
$$ {#eq-quadratic}

As seen in @eq-quadratic, there are two solutions.

### Text Formatting & Lists

You can make text italic or bold by enclosing it in single `*` or double `**` asterisks. 

Create an unordered list using the `-` symbol. For ordered lists, use numbers. You can actually just write `1.` for every item, and it will automatically order them correctly when the page renders.

```markdown
Make text *italic* or **bold**.

- Level 1 item
  - Level 2 item
    - Level 3 item

1. First element
1. Second element
1. Third element
```

Make text *italic* or **bold**.

- Level 1 item
  - Level 2 item
    - Level 3 item

1. First element
2. Second element
3. Third element

### Code Snippets

To format short inline code within a sentence, enclose the text in single backticks (\`). To insert multi-line blocks of code with syntax highlighting, enclose the block in triple backticks and specify the programming language on the first line.

````markdown
Use the `print()` function.

```cpp
int sum() {
  int a = 5;
  int b = 10;
  return a + b;
}
```
````

Use the `print()` function.

```cpp
int sum() {
  int a = 5;
  int b = 10;
  return a + b;
}
```

### Figures and Images

To insert an image, place the caption in square brackets and the file path in parentheses. You can also add cross-reference labels (`#fig-label`) and size controls (`width`) inside curly braces at the end of the image code.

```markdown
![Default frog picture.](/img/Examples/frog.png){#fig-frog width="80%"}

Look at @fig-frog for an example.
```

![Default frog picture.](/img/Examples/frog.png){#fig-frog width="80%"}

Look at @fig-frog for an example.

### Tables

Markdown tables require a header row and a divider (`|---|`). You can easily add a caption and a cross-reference label (`#tbl-label`) at the very bottom.

```markdown
| Column 1 | Column 2 | Column 3 |
|:---------|:---------|:---------|
| a        | b        | c        |
| d        | e        | f        |
: Table caption {#tbl-example}

Data is shown in @tbl-example.
```

| Column 1 | Column 2 | Column 3 |
|:---------|:---------|:---------|
| a        | b        | c        |
| d        | e        | f        |
: Table caption {#tbl-example}

Data is shown in @tbl-example.

### Blockquotes and Callouts

To emphasize a standard quote, use the `>` symbol at the start of the line. You can also use special callout blocks to draw attention to notes or warnings, created by opening and closing a block with three colons (`:::`).

```markdown
> "If it is on the internet... it must be true." - Abraham Lincoln

:::{.callout-note}
This is a helpful note! You can also use `.callout-warning` or `.callout-important`.
:::
```

> "If it is on the internet... it must be true." - Abraham Lincoln

:::{.callout-note}
This is a helpful note! You can also use `.callout-warning` or `.callout-important`.
:::

### Links and Citations

To insert a standard hyperlink, place the text in square brackets and the URL in parentheses. To cite a paper from our bibliography file, use the `@` symbol followed by the citation key inside square brackets.

```markdown
[GitHub Repository](https://github.com/MKReyesH/ScientificWiki)

The expansion of the universe is accelerating [@riess1998; @perlmutter1999].
```

[GitHub Repository](https://github.com/MKReyesH/ScientificWiki)

The expansion of the universe is accelerating [@riess1998; @perlmutter1999].
